description_long: This is a sleek, futuristic airplane with a streamlined, angular fuselage and a dark metallic finish. The wings are integrated into the body with a low-wing configuration, featuring pronounced, swept-back shapes that give it an aggressive, high-speed appearance. The engines are mounted on either side of the fuselage, appearing as large, armored pods that blend into the wing structure. The cockpit is positioned forward and is enclosed, with a subtle canopy that merges smoothly into the body. The tail section is sharply tapered, lacking a traditional vertical stabilizer, and instead features a low-profile, integrated design. The overall aesthetic suggests a high-performance, possibly military or advanced urban transport aircraft.
description: A dark, angular, futuristic airplane with integrated swept-back wings, side-mounted engines, and a forward enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated, streamlined fuselage, low-mounted swept wings, and large engine pods integrated into the wings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide stance of the engine pods, the narrow forward fuselage, and the subtle canopy of the cockpit.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, displaying the same streamlined body, wing integration, and engine pod details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the sharply tapered tail section, the absence of a traditional vertical stabilizer, and the broad, angular wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings integrated into the fuselage, no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted on either side of the fuselage, integrated into the wing structure.
attribute_tail_design: Low-profile, integrated tail with no traditional vertical stabilizer, featuring a sharply tapered rear fuselage.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear in the images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a streamlined canopy integrated into the fuselage, likely single-seat.
